title: Outreach
text: Our group is committed to translating research on urban soils into knowledge that is accessible to practitioners, planners, and the general public. Below is a selection of outreach activities and resources.

title: Outreach
text:
Policy & Technical Documents

SOON
CEDEUS Policy Brief · 2026
A technical document addressed to urban planners and municipal governments summarising evidence on how urban soil properties affect the performance of green infrastructure in Santiago’s Mediterranean climate. Developed in the context of the CEDEUS research agenda.
Download PDF

title: Projects
text:
Grant: Fondecyt Iniciación Funding: ANID Period: 2026 - 2029
Urban areas are major CO₂ emitters but also potential sinks through soil organic carbon (SOC) storage. Despite covering only ~2.5% of land, urban soils may store disproportionately high SOC, though estimates vary. However, the roles of soil texture and urban microclimate, particularly urban heat islands, remain poorly understood. This project examines how urban temperature and soil physical properties influence SOC dynamics in urban greenspaces in Santiago, Chile. By integrating field sampling, laboratory analyses and remote sensing the study aims to quantify SOC variability and identify mechanisms linking urban heat and soil properties, improving understanding of urban soils in climate mitigation strategies.

title: Urban Soil Biophysics
text:
Focus Areas


Soil Water Retention
Characterization of soil water retention curves (SWRCs) in urban soils.


Nature-Based Solutions
Hydrological performance of green infrastructure such as green roofs, urban parks, gardens, etc.


Urban Soil Health
Carbon dynamics, biological activity, and soil physical properties of urban soils.

title: Urban Soil Biophysics
text:
Institutional Context
We are part of CEDEUS: Centro de Desarrollo Urbano Sustentable. A research centre hosted jointly by Pontificia Universidad Católica de Chile and Universidad de Concepción, funded by ANID. Our work links soil biophysics with urban planning, climate adaptation, and environmental policy.
About our people →

title: People
text:
Assistant Professor · Facultad de Agronomía y Sistemas Naturales
Pontificia Universidad Católica de Chile
Researcher at CEDEUS. Working on the physical properties of urban soils, water retention, and the hydrological behaviour of the urban ecosystem.
